pattern: A string avoids both forbidden three-character subsequences only in a handful of shapes -- all zeros, all ones, exactly one 1 anywhere, or exactly two 1s sitting at the very first and last positions -- so the answer is just the smallest edit distance to any of those shapes, each computed with a closed-form count.
hints: Work out which shapes of string contain neither "011" nor "110" as a subsequence: having zero or one 1 anywhere is always safe, having every character a 1 is always safe, and otherwise the only safe shape is exactly two 1s, one at the very first character and one at the very last, with everything between them 0.

For each shape, the minimum flips needed is a direct count: turning everything to 0 costs the number of 1s; turning everything to 1 costs the number of 0s; keeping exactly one 1 costs (number of 1s - 1) if there is at least one 1 already (place the kept 1 where one already exists).

For the two-ends shape, count how many of the two endpoint characters are already 1 and how many of the interior characters are already 1, then combine those counts into the flip total; the final answer is the minimum across all the shapes you computed.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, flip an interior character
ones already sit at both ends with zeros between
alternating pattern with many forbidden subsequences
two interior ones with no ones at the boundary

stress.description: random binary strings across the length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minFlips(self, s):
        n = len(s)
        cnt1 = s.count('1')
        cost_all_zero = cnt1
        cost_all_one = n - cnt1
        cost_one_one = (cnt1 - 1) if cnt1 >= 1 else 1
        best = min(cost_all_zero, cost_all_one, cost_one_one)
        if n >= 2:
            a = 1 if s[0] == '1' else 0
            b = 1 if s[-1] == '1' else 0
            cost_ends = (1 - a) + (1 - b) + (cnt1 - a - b)
            best = min(best, cost_ends)
        return best
